data.title: AMA Research Finds Leadership Expectations Outpacing Organizational Support
data.description: In a recent report published on March 19, 2026, by the American Management Association (AMA), a renowned organization in professional development, it was highlighted that there is a concerning disparity between the expectations placed on leaders by organizations and the systems in place to assist them. The research sheds light on the widening gap in this regard, signifying a need for reassessment and improvement in the leadership support structures. This development underscores the critical importance of aligning leadership expectations with adequate support mechanisms for ensuring organizational success and effectiveness.

data.title: Save the Children US Named Warrior Insurance Network's 2026 Charitable Program Recipient
data.description: BEDFORD PARK, Ill. - A charitable initiative led by Warrior Insurance Network plans to provide assistance to Save the Children US in enhancing crucial youth services across the United States. Starting March 19, 2026, this initiative aims to amplify critical resources for American youth. WIN member insurance companies are spearheading this program, demonstrating a commitment to support Save the Children US's mission. This collaboration will help expand vital support for children in need nationwide.

data.title: Leading Kombucha Brand, Health-Ade, Partners with Lindsay Lohan in Playful New Campaign
data.description: In the city of Los Angeles, on March 19, 2026, a new project was launched under the direction of Jocelyn Cooper and production by Patricia Arquette. The creative aspects were handled by Juxtapose Studio. This endeavor led by Lohan aims to ignite a fresh trend of wellness. Health-Ade, renowned for their tasty and effervescent drinks that promote gut health, have been key players in this initiative.

data.title: In HelloNation, Real Estate Expert Sander Scott of Northport, MI, Explains Building Restrictions
data.description: In Northport, Michigan on March 19, 2026, the press release raises questions about land buying restrictions in Leelanau County. The article from HelloNation provides vital information on navigating property restrictions before building, including local zoning laws and environmental guidelines. This resource aims to assist buyers in avoiding unexpected obstacles.

data.title: NSU's College of Nursing Receives $650,000 in LINE Funding from the State of Florida
data.description: In Fort Lauderdale, Florida on March 19, 2026, the Florida Department of Education's LINE program awarded Nova Southeastern University's Ron and Assaf College of Nursing with $650,000. This grant aims to assist in addressing the increasing demand for nurses in the state of Florida.

data.title: Jack Henry Tap2Local™ Named "Small Business Payments Solution of the Year" in 2026 FinTech Breakthrough Awards Program
data.description: A renowned yearly awards program has acknowledged exceptional financial technology products and companies globally. MONETT, MO - On March 19, 2026 - Jack Henry® (Nasdaq: JKHY) unveiled that its Tap2Local™ innovation has been honored with the title of "Small Business Payments Solution of the Year".

data.title: Growth Protocol Partners with Databricks to Bring Explainable AI to Enterprise Data via Delta Sharing
data.description: Growth Protocol has introduced a new opportunity for enterprise clients to utilize its reasoning engine within Databricks through an SDK, all while avoiding the need to transfer any data. This partnership between Growth Protocol and Databricks was recently extended, offering significant benefits to businesses. The collaboration, which was announced on March 19, 2026 in New York, signifies a key development in the field of data and AI technology. This enhancement allows for seamless integration and utilization of Growth Protocol's tools within the Databricks platform, leading to improved efficiency and productivity for enterprises.

data.title: Elevate Named Best Consumer Payments Platform for Second Consecutive Year
data.description: Elevate's innovative cloud-based platform, powered by AI technology, has caught the attention of FinTech Breakthrough for revolutionizing the delivery and expansion of healthcare benefits. As of March 19, 2026, the company based in Denver has been acknowledged for its cutting-edge approach in transforming the administration of consumer-directed benefits. The platform's advanced capabilities have redefined the landscape of healthcare benefits, showcasing Elevate's commitment to modernizing the industry.

data.title: Pennsylvania Cybersecurity Firm Reclamere Marks 25 Years by Advancing Duty of Care Risk Standards
data.description: A cybersecurity company, Reclamere, located in Tyrone, Pennsylvania, has marked its 25th anniversary by spearheading a significant change in the way organizations in Pennsylvania and throughout the country manage cyber threats and responsibility. The firm was established in Central PA in 2001 and has since become a key player in the cybersecurity industry.

data.title: Harcum College Welcomes Paul Tomczak as IT Planning Executive Director
data.description: In Bryn Mawr, Pennsylvania, on March 19, 2026, Harcum College revealed the selection of Paul Tomczak as the new Executive Director for Enterprise Resource Planning. Starting his new role on March 23, 2026, Tomczak boasts over 20 years of leadership proficiency in enterprise software.

data.title: Covenant HR Announces Inclusion in Lockton & ASHHRA AP3 Partner Program as Healthcare Hiring Complexity Accelerates in 2026
data.description: In Holmes Beach, Florida, on March 19, 2026, a company named Covenant HR was chosen to be a part of the ASHHRA Preferred Partner Program (AP3). This program, managed in partnership with Lockton, aims to assist healthcare organizations in dealing with the growing challenges related to hiring complexities and risks anticipated in the year 2026. The selection of Covenant HR is a significant development in this initiative, highlighting their expertise and capabilities in this sector.

data.title: InVita's UDITracker Equips Hospital Teams to Stay Ahead of Rising Joint Commission Tracer Inspections
data.description: InVita Healthcare Technologies, a prominent supplier of specialized software solutions for Human Biologics, has introduced a new feature that consolidates tissue and implant records within the software. This functionality aims to assist hospital teams during tracer methodology reviews. The company made this announcement in Baltimore on March 19, 2026. This update is designed to streamline the documentation process and enhance efficiency, benefiting hospital staff involved in tracing methodology.

data.title: Fort Lee Dentist Restores Smile--and Hope--for Man Rebuilding His Life
data.description: In Fort Lee, New Jersey on March 19, 2026, a dental implant specialist is gaining recognition for showcasing compassion towards a man who was overcoming homelessness. Dr. Ari Levine, the proprietor of Universal Dental Implant Center in Fort Lee, recently took steps to improve the man's life.

data.title: SimpleChips Ships Industry’s First 1200V High-Voltage Monitor IC
data.description: SAN ANTONIO-- SimpleChips Technology Inc., a leading global provider of analog and mixed-signal ASICs for various industries, revealed at APEC 2026 in booth 2218 the launch of its new product, the 18SCT005. This innovative 1200V high-voltage monitor IC boasts exceptional output accuracy of 0.7%, making it the first of its kind in the industry. The monolithic IC is designed to cater to diverse applications such as automotive EV powertrains, inverters, and more.

data.title: Bleeding.org LIVE! is the First Celebrity-Driven National Event for the Bleeding Disorders Community
data.description: The National Bleeding Disorders Foundation (NBDF) and Talk About It are joining forces to bring a special event to screens across the country on March 28. In celebration of the 10th anniversary of Bleeding Disorders Awareness Month, this live presentation will feature music, personal stories, advocacy efforts, and educational content. This event aims to raise awareness about bleeding disorders and their impact. Scheduled for March 28, this program will be accessible to viewers nationwide.
